questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Natural selection operates at
questions.explanation: Natural selection operates at population level.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Find odd one w.r.t. population
questions.explanation: Natality, death rate and pyramid of age are related with survival.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following contributes an increase in population density?
questions.explanation: Natality leads to increase in population density.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is considered as more realistic growth model?
questions.explanation: More realistic growth model is logistic growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: a. Loss of unnecessary sense organs b. Pressence of adhesive organs c. Presence of suckers d. High reproductive capacity e. Well developed digestive system Choose correct option w.r.t. parasites

options.B: Only a, b, d & e are correct
options.C: Only a, b, c, d are correct

questions.explanation:
Parasites have adhesive organs, suckers, high reproductive capacity and complex life cycle but they also show
undeveloped digestive system and loss of unnecessory sense organs.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the incorrect match w.r.t. interspecific interaction Species A Species B Interaction
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Camouflage is an important mechanism where

options.A: Predators are never cryptically coloured for easy capturing of their prey
options.B: Prey species are cryptically coloured to avoid being detected easily by the predator
options.C: Prey produces some poisonous chemicals in their surroundings to protect themselves from predators
options.D: Predators evolved some physiological adaptations to reach to the prey present in some harsh habitats

questions.explanation:
Camouflage is an important mechanism where prey species are cryptically coloured to avoid being easily
detected by predator.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statement is correct ?

options.A: Two species within a given community can have exactly the same niche
options.B: Two species within a given community cannot have exactly the same niche
options.C: Two species can live permanently together

questions.explanation: Two species within a given community cannot have exactly the same niche but can live permanently together.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Organisms occupying similar ecological niche but different geographical areas of distribution are called
questions.explanation:
Organisms occupying similar ecological niche but in different geographical areas are called ecological
equivalents.
Edge species – The ecotone zone has greater number of species than either of two main communities.
Ecolines – Transitional zone between two diverse communities i.e. gradient of variations in ecotype in relation
to variation in ecological conditions.
Inquilines – An animal that lives habitually in the rest or abode of some other species.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Transition zone between two community is called
questions.explanation:
Transition zone between two community is called ecotone.
Ecoline – The intermediate form between two ecotypes
Buffer zone – Zone of biosphere reserve where limited human activities are allowed like education, research etc
Thermocline – It is equatic zone where temperature falls rapidly.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The taiga region is also known as
questions.explanation: Taiga region is also known as Northern conifer forest.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Read the following statements w.r.t. features of biomes and select the correct option for tropical rain forests a. Buttress roots b. Vines, lianas and epiphytes are abundant c. Highly leached soils d. Soil has high base content e. 30-40 m tall canopy structure with 2-3 strata only
questions.explanation: Tropical rain forests - Buttress roots, vines, lianas and epiphytes with highly leached soil.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A good soil is that which

options.A: Has very high water holding capacity
options.C: Has very low water holding capacity
options.D: Allows water to pass through it quickly

questions.explanation: A good soil is that which has moderate water holding capacity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Soil porosity is maximum in
questions.explanation:
Soil porosity
Sandy soil – Porous and loose
Clay soil – Abundant capillary pores
Silt soil – More porous
Loam soil – Moderate porous

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Rise in the temperature and air humidity can be observed from

options.B: Poles to equator region during latitudinal transition
options.C: Plains to mountain top during altitudinal movement
options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Rise in temperature and air humidity can be observed from poles to equator region during latitudinal transition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Match Column I with Column II and choose correct option Column I Column II (Depth of sea water) (Algae type) a. Shallowest depth (i) Brown algae b. Intermediate depth (ii) Green algae c. Greatest depth (iii) Red algae
questions.explanation:
Shallowest depth
–
Green algae
Intermediate depth
–
Brown algae
Greatest depth
–
Red algae

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A process which maintains constancy of internal body environment of organisms despite varying external environmental conditions is called as
questions.explanation:
Statement is related with homeostasis
Epistasis – Gene that mask the effect of another gene
Heterosis – Hybrid vigour i.e., superior than their parents
Antibiosis – Ammensalism (–; 0)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nearly all plants and an overwhelming majority (99 percent) of animals are categorised as

options.A: Conformers – They can maintain a constant internal environment
options.B: Conformers – They cannot maintain a constant internal environment
options.C: Regulators – They can maintain a constant internal environment
options.D: Regulators – They cannot maintain a constant internal environment

questions.explanation:
Nearly all plants and an overwhelming majority 99% of animals are conformers. They connot maintain a
constant internal environment.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Keolado national park is situated at ___ and is famous for ___.
questions.explanation: Keolado national park is situated at Bharatpur (Rajasthan) and is famous for Siberian Cranes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Altitude sickness which includes nausea, fatigue and heart palpitations occurs due to

options.A: High atmospheric pressure at high altitudes
options.B: Low atmospheric pressure at high altitude
options.C: High mountain height and high temperature
options.D: Heavy snowfall at high altitude and low temperature

questions.explanation:
Altitude sickness which includes nausea, fatigue and heart palpitations occurs due to low atmospheric pressure at
high altitude.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Consider the following statements (A - D) each with one or two blanks. (A) Some snails go into (i) to avoid (ii)_ related problems. (B) Since small animals have a (iii) surface area relative to their volume, they tend to lose body heat (iv) when it is cold outside. (C) Next to temperature, _(v) is the most ecologically important environmental factor. (D) Every (vi) the famous Keolado National Park in Rajasthan host thousands of migrating birds. Which one of the following option gives the correct fill ups for the respective blank numbers from (i) to (vi) in the statements?
questions.explanation: (i) Aestivation, (ii) Summer, (iii) Larger, (iv) Very fast, (v) Water, (vi) Winter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the correct statement w.r.t adaptations

options.A: Desert lizards bask in the sun and absorb heat when their body temperature drops below comfort zone
options.B:
To stop experiencing altitude sickness, the body compensates low oxygen availability by increasing binding
affinity of haemoglobin
options.C: Mammals of colder climate generally have larger ears and limbs to minimise heat loss
options.D:
Many desert plants have thin cuticle on their leaf surface and have their stomata arranged on upper surface
to minimise water loss

questions.explanation: Desert lizards bask in the sun and absorb heat when their body temperature drops below comfort zone.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the incorrect statement w.r.t. ecotypes

options.A: They are different from each other on the basis of morphological and physiological characters
options.D: There may be several ecotypes of the same organism

questions.explanation: Ecotypes are genetically dissimilar.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Altitude sickness is managed by the body by
questions.explanation: Altitude sickness is managed by the body by decreasing binding capacity of hemoglobin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A group of individuals living in a particular geographical area at a particular time is called
questions.explanation:
A group of individuals living in a particular geographical area at a particular time is called local population or
deme.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is not true for J-shaped growth curve?

options.B: Population never grows beyond carrying capacity

questions.explanation: Population never grows beyond carrying capacity for logistic growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The interaction between two living organisms of different species which is beneficial to both but is not obligatory because they can live without each other is known as
questions.explanation: Statement is related with proto-cooperation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Competion for food, light and space is most severe between two

options.A: Distantly related species growing in different habitat
options.B: Distantly related species growing in the same habitat
options.C: Closely related species growing in different habitat
options.D: Closely related species growing in the same area

questions.explanation:
Competion for food, space and light is most severe between two closely related species growing in the same
area.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following type of age pyramid reflects a stable population growth?
questions.explanation:
The age pyramid reflects a stable population growth is bell-shape e.g.
Post-reproductive
Reproduction
Pre-reproductive

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Biotic potential is

options.A: Intrinsic rate of natural increase under environmental limited condition
options.B: Intrinsic rate of natural increase under environmental unlimited condition
options.C: Extrinsic rate of natural increase under environmental limited conditions
options.D: Extrinsic rate of natural increase under environmental unlimited conditions

questions.explanation: Biotic potential is intrinsic rate of natural increase under environmental unlimited condition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cattle or goats are never browsing on Calotropis growing in abandoned fields because of the presence of
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pollination in Ophrys (a mediterranean orchid) occurs by
questions.explanation:
Pollination in Ophrys (orchid) occurs by insect (bee) through pseudocopulation mechanism. Here Ophrys
employs "sexual deceit".

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is most appropriately defined?

options.A:
Commensalism is a relationship in which one species is benefitted and the other is neither benefitted nor
harmed.
options.B: Parasite is an organism which always lives inside the body of other organism and may kill it
options.C:
Competion is defined as a process in which the fitness of one species is significantly higher in the
presence of another species
options.D: Mutualism is a relationship in which one species is benefitted whereas the other is unaffected

questions.explanation: Commensalism is a relationship in which one species is benefitted and other is neither benefitted nor harmed.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A predator

options.A: Is too efficient to overexploits its prey
options.B: Helps in maintaing species diversity by increasing the intensity of competion among prey species.
options.C: Acts as conduits for energy transfer across trophic levels

questions.explanation: A predator acts as conduit for energy transfer across trophic levels.

(d) Size of a population for any species is not a static parameter
questions.explanation:
Tiger census is often based on pug marks and fecal pellets; size of population for any species is not a static
parameter.

questions.explanation:

questions.subject: Biology

questions.explanation:
Competion is a rivalry between two or more organisms for obtaing the same resources. It is a negative

questions.explanation: Large woody vines called lianas commonly found in tropical rainforests.

questions.subject: Biology

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During DNA replication, Okazaki fragments are used to enlongate

options.A: The leading strand towards replication fork
options.B: The lagging strand towards replication fork
options.C: The leading strand away from replication fork
options.D: The lagging strand away from the replication fork

questions.explanation:
Two DNA polymerase molecules work simultaneous at the DNA fork, one on the leading strand and the other
on the lagging strand.
Each Okazaki fragment is synthesized by DNA polymerase at lagging strand in 5 3direction. New Okazaki
fragments appear as the replication fork opens further.
As the first Okazaki fragment appears away from the replication fork, the direction of elongation would be away
from replication fork.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Match the terms in Column I with their description in Column II and choose the correct option Column I Column II (a) Dominance (i) Many genes govern a single character (b) Codominance (ii) In a heterozygous organism only one alle expresses itself (c) Pleiotropy (iii) In a heterozygous organism both alles express themselves fully (d) Polygenic inheritance (iv) A single gene influences many characters
questions.explanation:
Dominance
-
Expression of only one alle in heterozygous organism.
Codominance
-
Side by side full expression of both alles. F1 resembles both parents.
Pleiotropy
-
Single gene can exhibit multiple phenotypic expression e.g., Phenyl ketonuria.
Polygenic inheritance
-
Many genes govern a single character e.g., Human skin colour.

col2.i: Many genes govern a single character
col2.ii: Expression of only one allele in heterozygous organism
col2.iii: Side-by-side full expression of both alleles
col2.iv: Single gene exhibits multiple phenotypic effects

questions.explanation: hCG and hPL are exclusively produced by placenta. hCG is functionally similar to LH.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following groups of hormones are produced in women only during pregnancy?
questions.explanation: hPL (= human placental lactogen) has lactogenic properties, thus supporting prolactin from mother's pituitary during pregnancy. hCG ( = human chorionic gonadotrophin) stimulates corpus luteum of pregnancy to continue secrete progesterone. Relaxin, secreted by corpus luteum, facilitates parturition by softening the connective tissue of public symphysis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Immediately after implantation, ectoderm, endoderm and the mesoderm of embryo is formed from
questions.explanation: Embryoblast is the name given to inner cell mass.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The stem cells which have potency to give rise to all tissues and organs are formed from
questions.explanation: Stem cells in inner cell mass form primary germinal layers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Foetal ejection reflex in human female is induced by
questions.explanation: The signals for parturition originate from fully developed foetus and the placenta which induce mild uterine contractions called foetal ejection reflex. This triggers release of oxytocin from the maternal pituitary.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Signals from fully developed foetus and placenta ultimately lead to parturition which requires the release of
questions.explanation: Oxytocin causes uterine contractions leading to child birth. Relaxin is released from corpus luteum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a set of male accessory ducts?

options.A: Rete testis, vasa efferentia, tubuli recti
options.B: Rete testis, vasa efferentia, epidymis and vas deferens
options.D: Seminiferous tubules, vasa efferentia, epidymis and vas deferens

questions.explanation: The male sex accessory ducts include rete testis, vasa efferentia, epidymis and vas deferens.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Trace the correct path of movement of the sperms upto urethra.

options.A: Seminiferous tubules Vasa efferentia Rete testis Epidymis Vas deferens Ejaculatory duct Urethra
options.B: Seminiferous tubules Rete testis Epidymis Vasa efferentia Vas deferens Ejaculatory duct Urethra
options.C: Seminiferous tubules Rete testis Vasa efferentia Epidymis Vas deferens Ejaculatory duct Urethra
options.D: Seminiferous tubules Rete testis Vasa efferentia Epidymis Ejaculatory duct Vas deferens Urethra

questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Given below is a diagrammatic sketch of a portion of human male reproductive system. Which of the following part contributes to the maximum portion of semen? Urinary bladder B C D Urethra Testis Foreskin Glans penis A
questions.explanation: Seminal vesicles contributes the major part of the ejaculate. C is prostate gland and D is bulbourethral glands.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following depicts the site of impantation of blastocyst under normal condition? IV III VI V I II
questions.explanation: I Endometrium II Myometrium III Infundibulum IV Fimbriae V Cervical canal VI Vagina

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is incorrect match?

options.A: Myometrium : Exhibits strong contractions during delivery of the baby
options.B: Endometrium : Undergoes cyclical changes during menstrual cycle

questions.explanation: The cavity of cervix is called cervical canal. Cervical canal + Vagina = Birth canal

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is the correct match about the female external genitalia and their functions?

options.A: Mons pubis : Cushion of fatty tissue covered by skin and pubic hair and surround the vaginal orifice
options.B: Labia majora : Fleshy folds of tissue which extend down from the mons pubis and surround the vaginal opening
options.C: Labia minora : Paired folds of tissue under the labia majora homologous to scrotum in males
options.D: Clitoris : A tiny finger like structure which lies at the upper junction of the two labia minora above the urethral opening. It is analogous to penis in males

questions.explanation: Labia majora surround vaginal opening and are homologous to scrotum. Clitoris is homologous to penis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The mammary glands are paired structures that contain the glandular tissue and variable amount of fat. The correct sequence of tissues involved in synthesis and flow of milk are

options.A: Mammary lobes Mammary alveoli Mammary ampulla Mammary duct Lactiferous duct
options.B: Mammary lobes Mammary alveoli Mammary duct Mammary ampulla Lactiferous duct
options.C: Mammary lobes Mammary alveoli Lactiferous duct Mammary ampulla Mammary duct
options.D: Mammary alveoli Mammary lobes Lactiferous duct Mammary duct

questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Study the flow chart. Name the hormones labelled as A, B, C, D at each stage. Hypothalamus Pituitary A Secrete B D C Leydig cells Sertoli cells acts at Secrete acts at Secrete Inhibin Choose the correct option. A B C D
questions.explanation: GnRH (= Gonadotropin releasing hormone) is a hypothalamic hormone, which acts on anterior pituitary to secrete gonodotropins (FSH & LH). LH acts at Leyding cells and stimulates synthesis and secretion of androgens. FSH acts on sertoli cells and stimulates some factors which help in the process of spermiogenesis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Given below are four statements (A - D) each with one or two blanks. Select the option which correctly fill up the blanks in two statements : A. The human male ejaculates about (i) million sperms during a coitus. Out of which, for normal fertility at least (ii) percent sperms must have normal shape and size B. A primary spermatocyte completes (i) meiotic division leading to the formation of two equal haploid cells called (i) C. Spermatogenesis starts at the age of (i) due to significant increase in the secretion of (ii) a hypothalamic hormone D. Oogenesis is initiated during embryonic development and at puberty only (i) primary follicles are left in each ovary

options.B: A - (i) : 200 to 300; (ii) 40 B - (i) : Second; (ii) Spermatids D - (i) : 60,000 to 80,000
options.D: C - (i) : Puberty; (ii) Gonadotropins C - (i) : Puberty; (ii) GnRH D - (i) : 60,000 to 80,000

questions.explanation: A (i) 200-300 million sperms; (ii) 60 B (i) First; (ii) Secondary spermatocyte C (i) Puberty; (ii) GnRH D (i) 60,000 to 80,000

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following labelled parts produces energy for the movement of the tail that facilitate sperm motility essential for fertilisation? A C Head D B
questions.explanation: A - Acrosome (has enzymes that help in fertilization) B - Nucleus (chromosomal material) C - Middle piece (having mitochondria and serves as energy source for swimming) D - Tail (for motility)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Androgen binding protein which helps in concentrating testosterone in the seminiferous tubule, and which inhibits ICSH secreted by anterior pituitary and GnRH production by hypothalamus is secreted by
questions.explanation: Sertoli cells are also known as nurse cells or sustentacular cells. They secrete inhibin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What induces the completion of the meiotic division of the secondary oocyte?

options.A: Contact of the sperm with the zona pellucida layer of the ovum
options.B: The entry of the sperm into the cytoplasm of the ovum through the zona pellucida and the plasma membrane
options.C: Entry of the sperm in the ampullary-isthmic junction

questions.explanation: The secretions of the acrosome help the sperm enter into cytoplasm of the ovum through the zona pellucida and the plasma membrane. This induces the completion of the meiotic division of the secondary oocyte.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The figure given below depicts a diagrammatic sectional view of ovary. Which one set of three parts out of I–VI are correctly identified? II III I Blood vessels V IV VI

options.A: VI - Primary follicle; III - Graafian follicle, V - Corpus luteum
options.B: II - Secondary follicle; III - Tertiary; IV - Ovulation
options.C: I - Primary follicle; II - Tertiary follicle; V - Corpus luteum
options.D: I - Primary follicle; II - Corpus luteum; V - Graafian follicle

questions.explanation: I - Primary follicle II - Tertiary follicle showing antrum III - Graafian follicle IV - Ovum V - Corpus luteum VI - Secondary follicle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is the incorrect match of the events occurring during menstrual cycle?

options.A: Menstruation : Breakdown of endometrium and ovum not fertilised
options.B: Ovulation : LH and FSH attain peak level
options.C: Proliferative phase : Rapid regeneration of endometrium and maturation of Graafian follicle
options.D: Development of corpus luteum : Follicular phase and increased secretion of progesterone

questions.explanation: Development of corpus luteum occurs in luteal or secretory phase.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the hormones that are secreted in large amount prior to ovulation : A. LH B. FSH C. Estrogen D. Progesterone
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Inhibition of uterine contraction ceases and the bleeding and cramps of menstruation begin due to
questions.explanation: Low level of progesterone causes menstruation because progesterone is responsible for maintaing endometrium of uterus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Why do all copulations not lead to fertilisation and pregnancy? The root cause is ___.

options.A: Due to numerous sperms and one ovum
options.C: Ovum and sperms are not transported simultaneously to the ampullary-isthmic junction
options.D: Due to non-formation of corpus luteum

questions.explanation: Life span of sperm in male reproductive tract few weeks Life span of sperm in female reproductive tract 48 to 72 hours. Viability of secondary oocyte after its release is for 24 hours. So, both must be viable for fertilization to occur.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Following the diagram of an ovum surrounded by few sperms : Sperm B Ovum C A Which of the following option is correct for the labelled parts A, B and C? A B C

options.A: Follicular cells Corona radiata Perivitelline space
options.B: Zona pellucida Perivitelline space Corona radiata
options.C: Zona pellucida Corona radiata Perivitelline space
options.D: Perivitelline space Zona pellucida Corona radiata

questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following enzyme helps sperm to penetrate zona pellucida?
questions.explanation: Acrosin is also called zona lysin and digests zona pellucida. Hyaluronidase dissolves hyaluronic acid. CPE dissolves corona radiata.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which centriole of spermatozoa is required for first cleavage?
questions.explanation: Proximal centriole and nucleus of sperm enter into the secondary oocyte during fertilization.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mark the incorrect statement

options.A: Polyspermy is prevented by depolarisation of the membrane is called as fast block
options.B: Entry of sperm into ovum restarts the cell cycle by breaking down MPF and turning on the APC
options.C: If implantation occurs anywhere else other than uterus, it is called tubal pregnancy
options.D: Ability to reproduce is lost in female primate after menopause

questions.explanation: Ectopic pregnancy is the term given for implantation occurring at site other than uterus. Implantation normally occurs in fundus part of uterus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Identify the stages A and B; and what is the correct labelling of C and D? A B C D Choose the correct option. A B C D

options.A: Morula Blastocyst Follicular cells Inner cell mass
options.C: Morula Blastocyst Trophoblast Inner cell mass
options.D: Blastocyst Morula Trophoblast Inner cell mass

questions.explanation: Blastomeres in the blastocyst are arranged into an outer layer called trophoblast and an inner group of cells attached to trophoblast called the inner cell mass.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first sign of the growing foetus may be noticed by listening to the heart sound carefully through the stethoscope. Embryo's heart is formed ___.

options.A: By the end of the second month of pregnancy
options.B: By the end of first trimester

questions.explanation: Factual information.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In human beings, pregnancy lasts for 9 months. The gestation period of dog, elephant, cat and cow is given below. Which of the following is wrong match? Animal Gestation period
questions.explanation: Cow's gestation period = 279 to 292 days.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The most toxic nitrogenous waste excreted by many bony fishes, aquatic amphibians and aquatic insects is
questions.explanation: The most toxic waste excreted by many bony fishes, aquatic amphibians and aquatic insects is ammonia; urea is comparatively less toxic and uric acid is least toxic.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In crustaceans, the excretory functions are performed by
questions.explanation: In crustaceans, excretory functions are performed by antennary or green gland.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is the largest digestive gland of our body?
questions.explanation: Liver is the largest digestive gland of the body.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Malpighian body or renal corpuscle is

options.D: Glomerulus along with Loop of Henle

questions.explanation: Malpighian body or Renal corpuscle = Glomerulus + Bowman's capsule

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Least toxic nitrogenous waste among the following is

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Ammonia > Urea > Uric acid

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The part through which arteries and veins enter or leave the kidney is called
questions.explanation: A depression where arteries and veins enter and leave body organ is known as hilum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Podocytes occur in
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Loop of Henle is found in
questions.explanation: Loop of Henle is found in nephron of the kidney of reptiles, birds and mammals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nitrogenous metabolic wastes in our body are the products of
questions.explanation: Nitrogenous wastes are the products of protein metabolism.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Human beings are
questions.explanation: Ureotelic – Human beings Uricotelic – Insects, birds Ammonotelic – Tadpole

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Uric acid is an excretory product of (a) Cockroach (b) Sparrow (c) Terrestrial reptiles (d) Man
questions.explanation: Uric acid : Insects, birds and terrestrial reptiles.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Kidneys are reddish brown, bean-shaped structures situated between the levels of ___ thoracic and ___ lumbar vertebrae.
questions.explanation: Kidneys are located between 12th thoracic and 3rd lumbar vertebra and is covered by peritoneum only on the front i.e. fused with the body wall. This arrangement is known as retroperitoneal arrangement.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: On an average, how much urea is excreted out per day by an adult human?
questions.explanation: 25–30 g of urea is excreted in urine per day. While normal blood urea level is 18–38 mg/100 ml.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An adult human excretes, on an average ___ litres of urine per day.
questions.explanation: Urine : • 1–1.5 L/day on an average • pH = 6.0 • Yellow coloured, watery • 25–30 g of urea is excreted out / day

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: All are performed in a nephron, except
questions.explanation: Urea synthesis occurs in the hepatocytes of liver via Ornithine cycle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is the first step of urine formation?
questions.explanation: Ultrafiltration is the first step of the urine formation. The blood is filtered inside the glomerular capsule. Filtrate = Blood – [Formed elements + Proteins] Filtrate = Plasma – Blood protein

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is the osmolarity of the filtrate at the hairpin bend of loop of Henle?
questions.explanation: Osmolarity of the filtrate at the hair pin bend of loop of Henle – 1200 mOsmL–1.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nearly all of the essential nutrients, and 70–80% of electrolytes and water are reabsorbed in the
questions.explanation: PCT : This is known as obligatory reabsorption.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following component of blood does not enter into the nephron?
questions.explanation: Plasma proteins are not filtered out during ultrafiltration in the glomerular capsule. Filtrate = Plasma – Plasma proteins.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The main function of loop of Henle is
questions.explanation: Loop of Henle, serves as the main function of conservation of water by countercurrent mechanism.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Maximum water reabsorption occurs in

options.D: Descending limb of loop of Henle

questions.explanation: 70–80% of electrolytes, all of the nutrients and most of the water are reabsorbed in the proximal convoluted tubule (PCT). This is obligatory reabsorption.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Vasa recta is
questions.explanation: Vasa recta is U-shaped in which blood flows in opposite direction to the flow of the filtrate in the loop of Henle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: We can produce concentrated urine. This is facilitated by a special mechanism. Identify the mechanism.

options.D: Counter current mechanism in Henle's loop/vasa recta

questions.explanation: Countercurrent mechanism : The arrangement of loop of Henle and the vasa recta in which opposite direction of flow of the filtrate and blood respectively facilitates increasing osmolarity towards the inner medullary interstium from 300 mOsmL–1 in the cortex to about 1200 mOsmL–1 in the inner medulla.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Glucose and amino acids in the filtrate are reabsorbed by tubular epithelial cells through
questions.explanation: Glucose and amino acids in the filtrate are reabsorbed in the proximal convoluted tubule via active transport.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The cause of glomerular filtration is
questions.explanation: Glomerular filtration pressure (GFP) which is 100 mmHg GFP = GHP – (BCOP + CHP) = 60 – (30 + 20) = 10 mmHg

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which segment of the nephron, reabsorption is minimum?
questions.explanation: Reabsorption is maximum in the PCT while reabsorption is minimum in ascending limb of the loop of Henle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is also known as antidiuretic hormone?
questions.explanation: Vasopressin is also known as ADH (antidiuretic hormone)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one is the vasoconstrictor?
questions.explanation: Vasoconstrictors : Angiotensin II Vasodilators : ANF, histamine Renin is also known as angiotensinogenase which catalyses the conversion of inactive angiotensinogen to angiotensin-II ultimately.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Hormone responsible for the absorption of water in DCT is
questions.explanation: ADH(antidiuretic hormone) is secreted by neurosecretory cells of hypothalamus which is released from posterior pituitary.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Aldosterone stimulates the reabsorption of
questions.explanation: Aldosterone is the hormone secreted by adrenal cortex and is helpful in the absorption of Na+ from renal tubules, thereby increasing the Na+ ion concentration in the blood.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Micturition is
questions.explanation: Micturition : Voiding of urine form the urinary bladder. Defaecation / Egestion : Removal of faecal matter Deamination : Removal of NH3

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The excretory structure of Amphioxus (Cephalochordate) is
questions.explanation: • Flame cells/Solenocytes – Amphioxus (Cephalochordates) • Coxal gland – Crustaceans • Malpighian tubules – Insects • Green gland – Crustaceans

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Blood which leaves liver and passes towards heart has higher concentration of
questions.explanation: Blood that leaves liver and passes to the heart has high concentration of urea because urea is synthesised in the hepatocytes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is excretory product of liver?

options.D: More than one option is correct

questions.explanation: Bilirubin and biliverdin are the excretory product of the liver formed by the destruction of Haemoglobin which are secreted in the bile.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Sweat contains
questions.explanation: Sweat contains : • Salt e.g., NaCl • Lactic acid • Small amount of urea

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: As compared to plasma, all are the constituents of dialysis fluid, except
questions.explanation: Urea is absent in the dialysing fluid because urea needs to be excreted out of the blood while other constituents are present in the dialysing fluid viz the solutes and electrolytes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The condition of accumulation of urea in blood is termed as
questions.explanation: Uremia : Condition of accumulation of urea in the blood. Diuresis : Increased excretion of urine. Glycosuria : Excretion of glucose in the urine. Haematuria : Presence of blood in the urine.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to the World Health Organisation (WHO), reproductive health means a total well-being in all aspects of reproduction, i.e., physical, emotional, behavioural and social. The headquarters of WHO are located in
questions.explanation: According to it, a society with people having physically and functionally normal reproductive organs and normal emotional and behavioural interactions among them in all sex-related aspects might be called reproductively healthy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: India was amongst the first countries in the world to initiate action plans and programmes at national level to attain reproductive health. The programmes called as 'family planning' were initiated in ___ year in India.
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: All the following are uses of amniocentesis, but one is misuse. Which one is misuse?

options.A: The centres for genetic counselling offer amniocentesis on request to women for chromosome analysis
options.B: This technique has been developed for detecting foetal abnormalities by analysing chromosomal defects
options.C: It is used to study metabolic defects of foetus like PKU (phenyl ketonuria)
options.D: It is done to examine the sex of the foetus leading to increasing female foeticides

questions.explanation: Amniocentesis is legal only for detection of any genetic disorder based on chromosomal pattern of foetus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to 2001 census, the population growth rate was
questions.explanation: Growth rate was about 2.1% per year during 1965-1970.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: World Population Day is
questions.explanation: 7th April World Health Day; 21st September - World Alzheimer's Day.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In India, marriageable age for boys is ___ and girls is ___ respectively.
questions.explanation: Marrying before the respective ages is illegal.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Among the following methods, which one has the highest failure rate?
questions.explanation: Rhythm method is periodic abstinence during fertile period (from day 10 to 17 of the menstrual cycle when ovulation could be expected) others are barrier methods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is not a natural method of contraception?
questions.explanation: Condoms are classified as barrier method. These also help in protection from sexually transmitted diseases.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Natural methods of contraception work on the principle of avoiding chances of ovum and sperm meeting. It includes A. Periodic abstinence B. Withdrawal C. Coitus interruptus D. Lactational amenorrhoea
questions.explanation: Periodic abstinence is natural method of avoiding coitus from day 10 to day 17 of menstrual cycle. Coitus interruptus or withdrawal is natural method in which the male partner withdraws his penis from the vagina just before ejaculation. Lactational amenorrhea is based on the fact that the menstrual cycle during intense lactation following parturition.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the rhythm method of birth control, the couple refrains from intercourse

options.A: One day before and after ovulation
options.B: Two days before and after ovulation
options.C: Three days before and after ovulation
options.D: One week before and after ovulation

questions.explanation: Day 10 to day 17 in a 28 day menstrual cycle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following method of contraception has maximum chances of failure?
questions.explanation: Rhythm/Periodic abstinence is natural method with failure rate of 20-30 per 100 women.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect statement for periodic abstinence?

options.A: The couple should abstain from coitus from day 10 to 17 of the menstrual cycle when ovulation could be expected
options.B: 10th to 17th day of the cycle is fertile period, when the chances of fertilisation are high
options.C: This prevents the chances of union of male and female gametes
options.D: In this method, the ovum and sperms are prevented from physically meeting with the help of barriers

questions.explanation: Periodic abstinence is a natural method, not a barrier method.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Lactational amenorrhoea, is a natural way of birth spacing. It is due to the high level of
questions.explanation: High level of prolactin inhibits the release of gonadotropins.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Reproductive health refers to

options.A: Total well being in all aspects of reproduction
options.B: Only the absence of reproductive diseases

questions.explanation: According to WHO, reproductive health means a total well-being in all aspects of reproduction, i.e., physical, emotional, behavioural and social.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The reproductive health in society can be improved by

options.B: Educating people about birth control methods
options.C: Educating people about sexually transmitted diseases

questions.explanation: All these methods help in creating awareness and improving reproductive health.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a natural method of contraception?
questions.explanation: Lactational amenorrhea is a natural method where ovulation and menstruation are absent during intense breastfeeding.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Periodic abstinence is a method in which couples

options.A: Avoid coitus from day 10 to 17 of the menstrual cycle
options.B: Avoid coitus from day 1 to 10 of the menstrual cycle
options.C: Avoid coitus from day 17 to 28 of the menstrual cycle

questions.explanation: Periodic abstinence or rhythm method involves avoiding coitus from day 10 to 17 of the menstrual cycle when ovulation is expected.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Coitus interruptus means
questions.explanation: Coitus interruptus or withdrawal method involves withdrawing the penis from the vagina just before ejaculation to prevent insemination.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a barrier method of contraception?
questions.explanation: Condom is a barrier method that prevents physical meeting of sperm and ovum.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Condoms are made of
questions.explanation: Condoms are generally made of latex or rubber.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is not an advantage of using condoms?
questions.explanation: Condoms are disposable and cannot be reused.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Diaphragms, cervical caps and vaults are placed in the
questions.explanation: These are barriers inserted into the vagina to cover the cervix.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a copper releasing IUD?
questions.explanation: CuT and Multiload 375 are copper releasing IUDs.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The IUD that releases hormones is
questions.explanation: LNG-20 is a hormone releasing IUD that releases levonorgestrel.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which IUD also acts as an emergency contraceptive?
questions.explanation: Copper releasing IUDs like CuT can be used as emergency contraceptives if inserted within 5 days of unprotected intercourse.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The mode of action of IUDs is
questions.explanation: IUDs act by releasing copper ions that suppress sperm motility, causing phagocytosis of sperms, and making the uterus unsuitable for implantation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Oral contraceptive pills contain
questions.explanation: Oral contraceptive pills contain small doses of progestogens or a combination of progestogens and estrogen.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Saheli is an oral contraceptive that contains
questions.explanation: Saheli is a non-steroidal oral contraceptive developed by CDRI, Lucknow.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The contraceptive pills inhibit
questions.explanation: Oral contraceptives inhibit ovulation by suppressing the release of FSH and LH.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an emergency contraceptive pill?
questions.explanation: Emergency contraceptive pills contain high doses of progestogen-estrogen combination and are used within 72 hours of unprotected intercourse.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Surgical methods of contraception include
questions.explanation: Vasectomy in males and tubectomy in females are surgical methods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Vasectomy involves

options.B: Cutting and tying of vas deferens

questions.explanation: Vasectomy is a surgical method where a small part of the vas deferens is removed or tied.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Tubectomy involves

options.B: Cutting and tying of fallopian tubes

questions.explanation: Tubectomy involves cutting and tying a small part of the fallopian tubes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) is legalized in India up to
questions.explanation: In India, MTP is legal up to 20 weeks of pregnancy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: MTPs are considered safe up to
questions.explanation: MTPs are considered relatively safe up to 12 weeks of pregnancy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an indication for MTP?

options.C: Risk to the mother or fetus

questions.explanation: MTP is performed in cases of contraceptive failure, rape, or when continuation of pregnancy poses a risk to the mother or fetus.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The main reason for population explosion in India is (1) Increase in death rate (2) Decrease in birth rate (3) Increase in number of people in reproductive age (4) Decrease in mortality rate

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The label of a herbarium sheet does not carry information on:
questions.explanation: Herbarium is a collection of plants that usually have been dried, pressed, preserved plant on sheets. It does not have information on height of the plant. [Source: NEET 2016, Phase II - Page 1, Q03]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Biological organisation starts with:
questions.explanation: Biological organisation starts with sub-microscopic molecular level, where four types of molecules, i.e. carbohydrates, lipids, proteins and nucleic acids are organised into organelles of cell. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 2, Q05]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The living organisms can be unexceptionally distinguished from the non-living things on the basis of their ability for:

options.B: interaction with the environment and progressive evolution

questions.explanation: All living organisms interact with their environment and shows progressive evolution. They can sense and respond to environmental cues. On the other hand reproduction, growth and movement cannot be all inclusive defining properties of living organisms. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 2, Q06]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Homeostasis is:

options.A: tendency to change with age in environment
options.D: plants and animals extracts used in homeopathy

questions.explanation: Homeostasis (Gr. homeos = similar; stasis = standing) is the tendency of maintaining a relatively stable internal physiological environment in an organism, or steady-state equilibrium in a population or ecosystem. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1991 - Page 2, Q12]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the correctly written scientific name of Mango which was first described by Carolus Linnaeus:
questions.explanation: The correct form of writing the scientific name of mango as described by Carolus Linnaeus is Mangifera indica Linn. As per binomial nomenclature rules, the name of an organism contains a generic and specific name. The former begins with capital letter while the later begins with small letter. [Source: NEET (National) 2019 - Page 3, Q14]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The size of Pleuropneumonia Like Organism (PPLO) is:
questions.explanation: The size of Pleuropneumonia Like Organism (PPLO) is 0.1 μm. Viruses are 0.02-0.2 μm, bacterial cell is 1-2 μm, eukaryotic cell is 10-20 μm. [Source: NEET (Oct.) 2020 - Page 4, Q02]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect about cyanobacteria?

options.C: They often form blooms in polluted water bodies
options.D: They have chlorophyll-a similar to green plants

questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria or blue-green algae have the ability of nitrogen fixation due to the presence of large pale cells called heterocyst in their filaments. Due to the presence of thick walls, heterocysts are impermeable to oxygen. [Source: NEET (Oct.) 2020 - Page 4, Q03]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Oxygen is not produced during photosynthesis by:
questions.explanation: Green sulphur bacteria are anaerobic bacteria. They do not evolve oxygen during photosynthesis. Such type of photosynthesis is known as anoxygenic photosynthesis. They do not use water as a source of reducing power. [Source: NEET 2018 - Page 4, Q04]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Methanogens belong to:
questions.explanation: Methanogens belong to archaeobacteria. It contains three major classes of primitive bacteria, i.e. methanogens, halophilic and thermoadipophilic. Methanogens are strict anaerobes, present in the gut of several ruminant animals. [Source: NEET 2016 Phase II - Page 5, Q10]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Chromatophores take part in:
questions.explanation: Chromatophores are found in members of phototrophic bacteria. They contain bacteriochlorophyll pigments and carotenoids and take part in photosynthesis. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2015 - Page 5, Q11]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nuclear membrane is absent in:
questions.explanation: Nostoc is a prokaryote. Prokaryotic cells lack membrane bound organelles and well organised nucleus, i.e. nuclear envelope is absent. Penicillium, Agaricus and Volvox are eukaryotic. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2012 - Page 6, Q17]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The primitive prokaryotes responsible for the production of biogas from the dung of ruminant animals, include the:
questions.explanation: Methanogens are group of obligate anaerobic ancient and primitive bacteria. They are involved in methanogenesis and produce methane gas in ruminant of cattle. [Source: NEET 2016 Phase I - Page 5, Q08]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A prokaryotic autotrophic nitrogen fixing symbiont is found in:
questions.explanation: The coralloid root of Cycas is symbiotically associated with nitrogen fixing blue-green algae, Anabaena cycadae and Nostoc punctiforme. These blue green-algae (cyanobacteria) are prokaryotic, photosynthetic and autotrophic. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2011 - Page 6, Q20]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ciliates differ from all other protozoans in:

options.B: having a contractile vacuole for removing excess water

questions.explanation: Ciliates differ from all other protozoans in having two types of nuclei. These two nuclei are usually of different size, i.e. one is meganucleus and the other is micronucleus. The former controls metabolism whereas the latter is concerned with reproductions. [Source: NEET 2018 - Page 9, Q47]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Chrysophytes, euglenoids, dinoflagellates and slime moulds are included in the kingdom:
questions.explanation: All single celled eukaryotic organisms like chrysophytes [diatoms and desmids], euglenoids [Euglena], dinoflagellates and slime moulds are included in kingdom-Protista. [Source: NEET 2016, Phase I - Page 9, Q48]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Pick up the wrong statement:

options.A: Cell wall is absent in Animalia
options.B: Protista have photosynthetic and heterotrophic modes of nutrition
options.D: Nuclear membrane is present in Monera

questions.explanation: Monera is the kingdom that contains unicellular organisms with a prokaryotic cell organisation, i.e. which lacks nuclear membrane and other membrane bound organelles. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2015 - Page 9, Q50]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is common about Trypanosoma, Noctiluca, Monocystis and Giardia?
questions.explanation: Trypanosoma, Noctiluca, Monocystis and Giardia are all unicellular protists. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 9, Q52]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: When a freshwater protozoan possessing a contractile vacuole is placed in a glass containing marine water, the vacuole will:
questions.explanation: Fresh water protozoans live in hypotonic environment so, for regulation of excess of water which comes in the protoplasm through the process of endosmosis, contractile vacuoles have developed. When these protozoans are placed in marine water, i.e. hypertonic water, the contractile vacuoles become disappear because the process of endosmosis does not occur. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2004 - Page 10, Q55]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which animal, dimorphic nucleus is found?
questions.explanation: Paramecium is heterokaryotic, it possesses a dimorphic nuclear apparatus (a single large macronucleus which controls metabolism and one or more small micronuclei concerned with reproduction). [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2002 - Page 10, Q57]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is wrong for fungi?

options.B: All fungi possess a purely cellulosic cell wall
options.D: They are both unicellular and multicellular

questions.explanation: In fungi, cell wall contains chitin or cellulose along with other polysaccharides, proteins and lipids. Only in some fungi, e.g. Phytophthora or other oomycetes, a purely cellulosic cell wall is present. [Source: NEET 2016, Phase II - Page 12, Q76]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The imperfect fungi which are decomposers of litter and help in mineral cycling belong to:
questions.explanation: The imperfect fungi which are decomposers of litter and help in mineral cycling belong to Deuteromycetes. They are fungi which do not fit into the commonly established taxonomic classification of fungi. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2015 - Page 12, Q78]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following algae contains mannitol as reserve food material?
questions.explanation: Ectocarpus is a cosmopolitan marine brown seaweed. Mannitol is stored as a food reserve in Ectocarpus. [Source: NEET 2021 - Page 18, Q01]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following algae produces carrageen?
questions.explanation: Carrageen is a common name give to polysaccharides (carbohydrates) that are extracted from seaweeds like red algae. Carrageen is known for its gelling properties. [Source: NEET 2021 - Page 18, Q02]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Phycoerythrin is the major pigment in:
questions.explanation: Phycoerythrin is the major pigment in red algae or rhodophytes. The photosynthetic pigments in red algae include chlorophyll-a, carotenoids and phycobilins. Phycoerythrin belongs to the phycobilins. [Source: NEET (Oct.) 2020 - Page 18, Q03]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Zygotic meiosis is characteristic of:
questions.explanation: Zygotic meiosis is represented in the haplontic life cycle of many algae including Chlamydomonas. In such a life cycle, all cells are haploid except zygote. [Source: NEET 2017 - Page 18, Q06]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An example of colonial alga is:
questions.explanation: Volvox is an example of colonial alga. [Source: NEET 2017 - Page 18, Q07]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following shows isogamy with non-flagellated gametes?
questions.explanation: Isogamy with non-flagellated gametes is seen in Spirogyra. It reproduces sexually by conjugation in which two non-flagellated morphologically similar but physiologically different gametes (isogamous) fuse together. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 19, Q10]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An alga which can be employed as food for human being is:
questions.explanation: Chlorella is a potential food source because it is high in protein and other essential nutrients when dried, it contains about 45% protein, 20% fat, 20% carbohydrate, 5% fibre and 10% minerals and vitamins. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 19, Q12]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Algae have cell wall made up of:
questions.explanation: Algae have cell wall made up of cellulose, galactans and mannans. Like plants, algae have cell walls containing either polysaccharides such as cellulose (a glucan) or a variety of glycoproteins or both. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2010 - Page 19, Q14]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mannitol is the stored food in:
questions.explanation: Fucus belongs to class-Phaeophyceae, in which reserve food is found in form of laminarin, mannitol and oil. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2009 - Page 20, Q15]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In case of poriferans, the spongocoel is lined with flagellated cells called:
questions.explanation: The body wall of a common sponge consists of three layer, i.e. pinacoderm, choanoderm and mesophyll layer. Choanoderm is inner cellular layer which consists of highly specialised flagellated cells called choanocytes. [Source: NEET 2017 - Page 31, Q01]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Body having meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food filtering flagellated cells and indirect development are the characteristics of phylum:
questions.explanation: In Porifera (sponges), bodies are asymmetrical. Body lacks tissue or organs, but from a meshwork of cells surrounding channels that open to the outside through pores, and that expand into internal cavities lined with food filtering flagellated cells (choanocytes). [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2015 - Page 31, Q02]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Sycon belongs to a group of animals which are best described as:

options.B: multicellular having tissue organisation, but no body cavity

questions.explanation: Sycon belongs to phylum-Porifera. The porifers are most primitive group of multicellular animals. They have no tissue grade of organisation and represent cell aggregated body plan, hence, included in the sub-kingdom-Parazoa. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2003 - Page 31, Q04]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The canal system is a characteristic feature of:
questions.explanation: Sponges possess an extensive system of interconnected cavities called canal system, which typically consists of incurrent canals, radial canals, excurrent canals and spongocoel. The system is useful for nutrition, respiration and excretion. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1999 - Page 31, Q05]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is true about all sponges without exception?

options.C: They have a mixed skeleton consisting of spicules and spongin fibres
options.D: They reproduce only asexually by budding

questions.explanation: Sponges are all aquatic, mostly marine but few fresh water forms also exist. Choanocytes or collar cells are only present in sponges. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1996 - Page 31, Q06]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Classification of Porifera is based on:
questions.explanation: Classification of sponges is primarily based on skeleton which are spicules. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1991 - Page 32, Q08]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following living organisms completely lacks a cell wall?
questions.explanation: Sea-fan (Gorgonia) belongs to kingdom - Animalia, phylum-Cnidaria. As it is an animal, thus it lacks cell wall unlike cyanobacteria or blue-green algae (kingdom-Monera) have cell wall composed of peptidoglycan. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 32, Q09]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Select the taxon mentioned that represents both marine and freshwater species:
questions.explanation: Cnidarian members are found both in freshwater and marine environments, while members of Ctenophora, Cephalochordata and Echinodermata are found exclusively in marine environment. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 32, Q10]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which animal nerve cell is present but brain is absent?
questions.explanation: The neurons of Hydra synapse with each other and other body cells. As a result, it responds to external stimuli. However, there is no brain in Hydra to coordinate the responses. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2002 - Page 32, Q11]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In Hydra, waste material of food digestion and nitrogenous waste material are removed from:
questions.explanation: In Hydra, undigested residues are egested from coelenteron through mouth, while nitrogenous excretory product (ammonia) is removed through general body surface. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2001 - Page 32, Q12]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Most appropriate term to describe the life cycle of Obelia is:
questions.explanation: True alternation of generations is alternation between haploid gametophyte and diploid sporophyte phase. In Obelia, the asexual polypoid phase is alternate with sexual medusoid phase and this type of life cycle is called metagenesis. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2002 - Page 32, Q13]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Special character of coelenterates is:
questions.explanation: The most characteristic feature of coelenterates is the presence of nematocysts or stinging cells. Nematocysts are mainly concerned with food capture, defence and attachment. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1994 - Page 32, Q15]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Biradial symmetry and lack of cnidoblasts are the characteristics of:
questions.explanation: Ctenoplana and Beroe lack cnidoblasts and have biradial symmetry. These belong to phylum-Ctenophora. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 33, Q20]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bilaterally symmetrical and acoelomate animals are exemplified by:
questions.explanation: Platyhelminthes are bilaterally symmetrical, triploblastic and acoelomate animals with organ level of organisation. [Source: NEET (Sep.) 2020 - Page 33, Q22]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Planaria possess high capacity of:
questions.explanation: Planaria is a flatworm belonging to phylum-Platyhelminthes. They are the simplest form of multicellular animal. They have high capacity of regeneration due to the neoblast cells. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 33, Q23]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: One example of animal having a single opening to the outside that serves both as mouth as well as anus is:
questions.explanation: These worms have incomplete alimentary canal, there is a single opening for both ingestion and egestion. This is also called as blind sac body plan. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2010 - Page 33, Q24]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about certain given animals is correct?
questions.explanation: Round worms (phylum-Aschelminthes) are pseudocoelomates, false coelom is derived from embryonic blastocoel. Flat worms (phylum-Platyhelminthes) are acoelomate animals. Molluscs and insects (phylum-Arthropoda) are coelomate animals. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014 - Page 34, Q33]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following groups of animals is bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic?
questions.explanation: Aschelminthes are bilateral symmetrical and triploblastic animals, e.g. Ascaris. Coelenterates are radially symmetrical and diploblastic. Ctenophores are radially symmetrical and diploblastic. Sponges are asymmetrical or radially symmetrical and diploblastic. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2009 - Page 34, Q34]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ascaris is characterised by:

options.A: absence of true coelom but presence of metamerism
options.B: presence of neither true coelom nor metamerism
options.C: presence of true coelom but absence of metamerism
options.D: presence of true coelom and metamerism

questions.explanation: Ascaris is endoparasite of man. There is no metameric segmentation. In Ascaris between body wall and visceral organs is a spacious fluid filled cavity. This cavity is not true coelom as it is not lined by coelomic epithelium. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2008 - Page 34, Q35]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is common between Ascaris lumbricoides and Anopheles stephensi?
questions.explanation: Both A. lumbricoides and A. stephensi have different males and females and it is possible to distinguish between them morphologically. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2000 - Page 35, Q36]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following options does correctly represent the characteristic features of phylum-Annelida?

options.A: Triploblastic, unsegmented body and bilaterally symmetrical
options.B: Triploblastic, segmented body and bilaterally symmetrical
options.C: Triploblastic, flattened body and acoelomate condition
options.D: Diploblastic, mostly marine and radially symmetrical

questions.explanation: Animals belonging to phylum-Annelida are triploblastic, bilaterally symmetrical and metamerically segmented. They exhibit organ system level of body organisation with presence of coelom. [Source: NEET (Oct.) 2020 - Page 35, Q40]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: If a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging its gut, the fluid that comes out is:
questions.explanation: The body cavity of earthworm is true coelom (schizocoel) as it is formed by the division of mesoderm. The coelom is filled with milky, alkaline coelomic fluid, which contains different types of corpuscles. Thus, if alive earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface, the coelomic fluid will come out. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2009 - Page 35, Q44]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is not a characteristic of phylum-Annelida?
questions.explanation: The body of annelids is elongated, bilaterally symmetrical, triploblastic, truly coelomate and metamerically segmented into similar metameres. The coelom is true, schizocoelous. Pseudocoelom is the body cavity of Aschelminthes (roundworm). [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2008 - Page 36, Q45]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Earthworms have no skeleton but during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and acts as a hydraulic skeleton. It is due to:
questions.explanation: The body cavity (coelom) of earthworm is filled with an alkaline, colourless or milky coelomic fluid containing water, salts some proteins and four types of coelomic corpuscles. During burrowing the coelomic fluid becomes turgid and acts as hydraulic skeleton. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2008 - Page 36, Q46]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is correct matching pair of a body feature and the animal possessing it?

options.C: Pharyngeal gills slits absent in embryo - Chameleon

questions.explanation: The nervous system of leech consists of ventral central nervous system, peripheral nervous system and sympathetic nervous system. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 36, Q47]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Functionwise, just as there are nephridia in an earthworm, so are:
questions.explanation: Flame cells in liver fluke are excretory organs as nephridia in an earthworm. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1996 - Page 36, Q49]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: True coelom is the space lying between the alimentary canal and body wall enclosed by the layers of:

options.B: endoderm on one side and ectoderm on the other
options.C: mesoderm on one side and ectoderm on the other

questions.explanation: Coeloms are secondary body cavities bounded on all sides by mesodermal peritoneum. The true coelom arises within the mesoderm itself. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1996 - Page 36, Q50]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following belongs to the family-Muscidae?
questions.explanation: Housefly belong to Muscidae family. Muscidae are a family of flies found in superfamily-Muscidae. [Source: NEET 2021 - Page 37, Q57]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which group of animals belong to the same phylum?
questions.explanation: Prawn, Scorpion, Locusta belong to phylum-Arthropoda. Malarial parasite (Plasmodium vivax) and Amoeba belong to phylum-Protozoa. Mosquito-Phylum-Arthropoda. Earthworm-Phylum-Annelida. Pinworm and Tapeworm-Phylum-Aschelminthes. Sponge-Phylum-Porifera, Sea anemone-Phylum-Coelenterata, Starfish-Phylum-Echinodermata. [Source: NEET 2013 - Page 37, Q61]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: One of the representative of Phylum-Arthropoda is:
questions.explanation: Silver fish- Arthropoda (phylum). Silver fish is a small, wingless insect in the order Thysanura. Cuttle fish- Mollusca. Puffer fish- Chordata (phylum), class-Pisces. Flying fish- Pisces. [Source: NEET 2013 - Page 37, Q62]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following have the highest number of species in nature?
questions.explanation: More than 70% of all the species recorded are animals. Among animals, insects are the most species rich taxonomic group, making more than 70% of the total. It means out of every 10 animals on this planet, 7 are insects. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2011 - Page 38, Q64]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is correctly states as it happens in the common cockroach?

options.A: Oxygen is transported by haemoglobin in blood
options.C: The food is ground by mandibles and gizzard
options.D: Malpighian tubules are excretory organs projecting out from the colon

questions.explanation: In cockroach, mandibles are a pair of hard, strong, large, dark coloured triangular structures which move in horizontal motion and crush food between them. Gizzard or proventriculus has an outer layer of thick circular muscles and thick inner cuticle forming six highly chitinous plate called teeth. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2011 - Page 38, Q65]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is true about Nereis, scorpion, cockroach and silver fish?

options.A: They all have jointed paired appendages
options.D: They all belong to the same phylum

questions.explanation: Nereis living in burrows in sand or mud often with clams. Scorpion are abundant in deserts. Cockroach are found in warmth, dampness and plenty of organic food to devour. Silver fish residing in damp cool places. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 39, Q69]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Two common characters found in centipede, cockroach and crab are:
questions.explanation: Crab, centipede and cockroach belong to phylum-Arthropoda. These have jointed appendages and chitinous exoskeleton. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 39, Q70]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In Arthropoda, head and thorax are often fused to form cephalothorax, but in which one of the following classes, is the body divided into head, thorax and abdomen?
questions.explanation: An arthropod body consists of head, head and thorax in some cases cephalothorax. Class-Insecta have body divided into head, thorax and abdomen. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2004 - Page 39, Q72]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What is common among silver fish, scorpion, crab and honeybee?
questions.explanation: The main characteristics of phylum-Arthropoda are jointed appendages, present in some or all somite or segments, but often modified for specialised functions like walking, clinging, jumping, etc. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1997 - Page 39, Q74]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A larval stage occurs in the life history of all members of the group:
questions.explanation: Lizard, cockroach, Ascaris, earthworm shows direct development in their life cycle. Whereas, butterfly have caterpillar, frog have tadpole and mosquito have wriggler larvae in their life cycle. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1993 - Page 39, Q75]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Adult Culex and Anopheles can be distinguished with the help of:
questions.explanation: The body of Anopheles mosquito makes an angle of 45° while sitting whereas the body of Culex mosquito lies parallel to the surface. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1992 - Page 39, Q76]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Malpighian tubules are:
questions.explanation: Malpighian tubules are the excretory organs of insects. These are unbranched tubules lying almost freely in haemocoel and open into alimentary canal. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1990 - Page 40, Q81]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which one of the following, the genus name, its two characters and its phylum are not correctly matched, whereas the remaining three are correct?

options.A: Pila - Body segmented, Mouth with Radula - Mollusca
options.B: Asterias - Spiny skinned, Water vascular system - Echinodermata
options.C: Sycon - Pore bearing, Canal system - Porifera
options.D: Periplaneta - Jointed appendages, Chitinous exoskeleton - Arthropoda

questions.explanation: Option (a) is incorrect because molluscs are bilaterally symmetrical, triploblastic, coelomate, soft bodied animals. Their soft body is covered by a calcareous shell and is unsegmented with a distinct head, muscular foot and visceral hump. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2012 - Page 40, Q85]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following is a matching set of phylum and its three examples?
questions.explanation: Loligo, Teredo and Octopus are the members of phylum-Mollusca. Loligo is commonly called squid. Octopus (devil fish) is found at the bottom of the sea. Teredo or shipworm is a marine bivalve. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 40, Q86]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Closed circulatory system occurs in:
questions.explanation: Closed circulatory system occurs in cuttle fish, i.e. Sepia, cephalopods are the only molluscs with a closed circulatory system. They have two gill hearts that move blood through capillaries of the gill. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1994 - Page 41, Q87]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In bryophytes, the gemmae help in which one of the following?
questions.explanation: Gemmae are green, multicellular, asexual buds which develop in small receptacles called gemma cups and help in asexual reproduction in bryophytes. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 2, Q102]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a zygomorphic flower?
questions.explanation: Zygomorphic flowers can be divided into two equal halves by only a single vertical plane and shows bilateral symmetry. Pea possess zygomorphic flowers. Chilli, Petunia and Datura possess actinomorphic flowers. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 4, Q106]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Who proposed that the genetic code for amino acids should be made up of three nucleotides?
questions.explanation: George Gamow, a physicist proposed that genetic code for amino acids should be made up of three nucleotides. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 4, Q107]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Sweet potato and potato represent a certain type of evolution. Select the correct combination of terms to explain the evolution.
questions.explanation: Sweet potato is a root modification while potato is a stem modification but both of them have same function. Analogous structures are not anatomically similar structures though they perform similar functions. Analogous structures are the result of convergent evolution. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 5, Q109]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Histones are enriched with:
questions.explanation: In eukaryotes, packaging of DNA is much more complex. There is a set of positively charged, basic proteins called histones. They are rich in the basic amino acid residues lysine and arginine. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 5, Q111]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following equations represents the Verhulst-Pearl Logistic Growth of population?
questions.explanation: Logistic growth is described by Verhulst-Pearl logistic growth equation dN/dt = rN[(K-N)/K]. [Source: NEET (UG)-2025 (Code-47) - Page 5, Q112]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion (A), and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

options.A: : The primary function of the Golgi apparatus is to package the materials made by the endoplasmic reticulum and deliver it to intracellular targets and outside the cell.

questions.explanation: In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer:

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about RuBisCO is true?

options.A: It catalyzes the carboxylation of RuBP
options.B: It is active only in the dark
options.C: It has higher affinity for oxygen than carbon dioxide
options.D: It is an enzyme involved in the photolysis of water
