questions.explanation: Abscisic acid promotes reversal of H⁺⇌K⁺ pump and increasing availability of H⁺ inside the guard cell cytoplasm. Loss of K⁺ decreases osmotic concentration of guard cells as compared to adjacent epidermal cells. Due to the exosmosis the turgidity of guard cells decreases and it closes the pore of stoma. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1994 - Page 144, Q18]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following growth regulators is known as stress hormone?
questions.explanation: Abscisic Acid (ABA) is also known as 'stress hormone' or dormin because it is produced in much higher amounts, when plants are subjected to various kinds of stresses. It often gives plant organs a signal that they are undergoing physiological stresses. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2014,1993 - Page 144, Q19]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During seed germination its stored food is mobilised by:
questions.explanation: Gibberellin induces aleurone cells to secrete enzyme that break stored food in seed. Cytokinins promote nutrient mobilisation which helps in the delay of leaf senescence. [Source: NEET 2013 - Page 144, Q20]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Phototropic curvature is the result of uneven distribution of:
questions.explanation: Darwin and his son Francis used germinating oat and canary grass seedlings in their experiments and hypothesised that when shoots were illuminated from one side, they bent forward the light in response to an 'influence', that was transmitted downward from its source at the tip of the shoot. The compound (auxin) found by Went is relatively abundant in coleoptile tips. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2010 - Page 144, Q21]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Coiling of garden pea tendrils around any support is an example of:
questions.explanation: Thigmotropism movements are due to the contact with a foreign body. In twiners and lianas, there is less growth on the side of contact and more growth on the side of branch away from the contact. Coiling of garden pea tendrils around any support is an example of thigmotropism. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2010,1995,91 - Page 144, Q22]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: One of the synthetic auxin is:
questions.explanation: NAA (Naphthalene Acetic Acid) and 2,4-D (2,4-dichlorophenoxy acetic acid) are synthetic auxins. The term auxin is applied to the indole-3-acetic acid (IAA) and to other natural and synthetic compounds having certain growth regulating properties. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2009 - Page 144, Q23]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following acids is a derivative of carotenoids?
questions.explanation: Abscisic acid is a terpenoid, which is a derivative of steroid (carotenoid). Indole butyric acid and indole-3-acetic acid are auxins which are weak organic acids. Gibberellic acid (gibberellin) is a terpene. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2009 - Page 145, Q24]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Opening of floral buds into flowers, is a type of:
questions.explanation: Opening of floral buds into flower is a type of autonomic movement of growth (nastic motion). This is non-directional movement in which the response is determined by the structure of the responsive organ and not to the direction of stimulus. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 145, Q25]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: 'Foolish seedling' disease of rice led to the discovery of:
questions.explanation: Gibberellins (GA) were first observed from the fungus Gibberella fujikuroi, the causal organism of foolish seedling disease of rice plants in Japan by Kurosawa in 1926. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 145, Q26]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which one of the following pairs, is not correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Gibberellins help in cell growth of stem, leaves and other aerial parts. They are not involved in leaf fall. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2007 - Page 145, Q27]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: How does pruning help in making the hedge dense?

options.A: It free axillary buds from apical dominance
options.B: The apical shoot grows faster after pruning
options.D: It induces the differentiation of new shoots from the rootstock

questions.explanation: Pruning helps in making the hedge dense as it free axillary buds from apical dominance. In fact, the apices of the plant axis has the highest concentration of auxin which suppresses the axillary buds while promotes the growth of apical bud. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 145, Q28]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An enzyme that can stimulate germination of barley seeds is:
questions.explanation: Barley seeds are rich in carbohydrate (starch). The starch is hydrolysed by α-amylase to monosaccharides unit at the time of germination of seeds. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 145, Q29]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Treatment of seed at low temperature under moist conditions to break its dormancy is called:
questions.explanation: Stratification involves the treatment of seed at low temperature (5-10°C) under sufficiently moist conditions to break its dormancy and to induce germination. Scarification involves any damage or breakage of seed coat by physical methods. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2006 - Page 145, Q30]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cell elongation in internodal regions of the green plants takes place due to:
questions.explanation: Gibberellin (GA) promotes internodal elongation in a wide range of species. This internodal elongation phenomenon is known as bolting. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2004 - Page 145, Q31]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Differentiation of shoot is controlled by:
questions.explanation: Ratio of cytokinins to auxins controls differentiation. If there is more cytokinin than auxin, shoot buds develop. If there is more auxin than cytokinin, roots develop. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2003 - Page 145, Q32]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Plants deficient of element zinc, show its effect on the biosynthesis of plant growth hormone:
questions.explanation: Deficiency of zinc effects biosynthesis of auxin. It is characterised by a reduction in internodal growth due to which plant develops in rosette habit. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2003 - Page 145, Q33]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Hormone responsible for senescence is:
questions.explanation: ABA is a plant growth inhibitor and an inhibitor of plant metabolism. It is responsible for senescence. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2001 - Page 145, Q34]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following prevents fall of fruits?
questions.explanation: NAA (Naphthalene Acetic Acid) is a synthetic auxin hormone which is useful for preventing pre-harvest fruit drop of tomatoes. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2001 - Page 146, Q35]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which breaks bud dormancy of potato tuber?
questions.explanation: Gibberellins overcome the natural dormancy of buds, seeds, tubers, etc. In this way, these are antagonistic to ABA. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2001 - Page 146, Q36]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What reason will you assign for coconut milk used in tissue culture?
questions.explanation: Skoog (1954-1956) observed that coconut milk contained a substance which stimulated cell division. The substance was later on called cytokinin. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 2000,03 - Page 146, Q37]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The closing and opening of the leaves of Mimosa pudica is due to:
questions.explanation: Seismonastic movements are nastic movements of turgor in response to stimulus of shock (like touch/mechanical/electrical/thermal/chemical shock). On touching Mimosa pudica, its leaves droop down. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1999 - Page 146, Q38]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which combination of gases is suitable for fruit ripening?
questions.explanation: Ethylene is a gaseous hormone which promotes ripening of fruits. Methionine amino acid is precursor molecule for ethylene synthesis. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1998 - Page 146, Q39]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A plant hormone used for inducing morphogenesis in plant tissue culture is:
questions.explanation: Ratio of cytokinin to auxin controls cell differentiation. If there is more cytokinin than auxin, shoot buds develop. Relatively more auxin than cytokinins leads to the development of roots. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1998 - Page 146, Q40]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gibberellins induce:

options.B: production of hydrolysing enzymes in germinating seeds

questions.explanation: During seed germination especially of cereals gibberellins stimulate the production of hydrolytic enzymes like amylases, lipases, ribonucleases. These enzymes solubilise the reserve food of the seed. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1997 - Page 146, Q41]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ethylene gas is used for:
questions.explanation: Climacteric fruits are fleshy fruits which show a sudden sharp rise of respiration rate at the time of ripening. Ethylene is used to induce artificial ripening of these fruits, e.g. apple, mango, banana. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1995 - Page 146, Q42]

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Movement of auxin is:
questions.explanation: Went (1928) reported that auxin is transported basipetally, i.e., it moves from apical to basal end. However, McCready and Jacobs (1963) working on petiole segments observed acropetal movement of auxin. Recent studies have indicated that the polar movement of auxin is an active transport. [Source: CBSE AIPMT 1994 - Page 146, Q43]
